money_flow_summary: Money flows from patients → insurers → providers, with pharmaceutical companies and device makers extracting value upstream. The solo-entrepreneur opportunity avoids the regulated clinical pathway: focus on health administration automation, wellness tools, and professional productivity for healthcare workers — all areas where AI delivers value without requiring FDA approval.

top_opportunities.title: AI Medical Scribe for Private Practice (Ambient Documentation)
top_opportunities.how_money_is_made: Build an ambient AI scribe that listens to doctor-patient conversations (with consent), transcribes, and generates structured SOAP notes in the format the doctor's EHR requires. Export directly to Epic/AthenaHealth via HL7/FHIR API. Charge $199-$499/mo per provider. Doctors spend 2+ hours/day on documentation — AI scribes buy back that time. Suki AI charges $249/mo; the market is far from saturated.
top_opportunities.moat: EHR integration complexity creates barrier; doctor workflow familiarity creates stickiness
top_opportunities.first_step: Build mobile audio capture + Whisper transcription + SOAP note generation with Claude; integrate with AthenaHealth API; get 5 beta doctors from medical school network
top_opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI listens to natural conversation and produces structured clinical documentation — capturing diagnoses, symptoms, treatment plans, and follow-up tasks without the doctor touching a keyboard

top_opportunities.title: Prior Authorization AI Agent for Medical Practices
top_opportunities.how_money_is_made: Prior authorization is a $25B+ administrative burden on medical practices. Build an AI agent that reads the insurance plan requirements, gathers clinical documentation from the EHR, fills out the PA form, submits electronically, and tracks status. Charge $299-$999/mo per practice. A typical primary care practice spends $15,000-$30,000/yr on prior auth — this is a clear ROI sale.
top_opportunities.moat: Insurance plan requirement database grows over time; EHR integrations create high switching cost
top_opportunities.first_step: Build a scraper for CMS prior auth requirements; integrate with AthenaHealth patient data API; demo with 1 medical practice handling 20+ PAs per week
top_opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI reads unstructured insurance coverage documents, matches them to patient clinical data, and fills complex forms autonomously — a task that currently takes 30-90 minutes per authorization

top_opportunities.title: Healthcare Revenue Cycle Denial Management Agent
top_opportunities.how_money_is_made: Hospitals and clinics lose $262B/yr to denied insurance claims, most of which are recoverable with the right appeal. Build an AI agent that reads denial EOB notices, identifies the denial reason, writes an appeal letter with supporting clinical documentation, and submits electronically. Charge either $299-$999/mo or 10% of recovered revenue. Even recovering 5% of denials for a $5M/yr practice = $250k/yr.
top_opportunities.moat: Denial pattern database per insurance company creates competitive advantage over time
top_opportunities.first_step: Analyze 100 real denial EOBs to identify top 10 denial reason codes; build appeal letter templates; find 1 medical billing company to pilot with
top_opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI reads the insurance company's denial reason and generates a medically and legally appropriate appeal that references specific CPT codes, ICD-10 diagnoses, and payer-specific guidelines
